description_long: This is a compact, two-story brick house with a steep gable roof covered in weathered metal sheeting. The exterior walls are primarily yellow-brown brick, with a wooden extension on one side featuring a sloped roof and a staircase leading to a side entrance. The house has several windows, including tall arched windows on the main brick facade and rectangular windows on the wooden extension. A metal railing borders the staircase, and there are visible utility fixtures such as a wall-mounted lamp and antenna. The structure appears solid and utilitarian, with minimal decorative elements, suggesting a practical urban or railway-adjacent function.
description: A two-story yellow-brown brick house with a gable metal roof, wooden side extension, and external staircase. Features arched and rectangular windows, a metal railing, and utility fixtures.
description_view_0: Shows the narrow brick facade with an arched window, a red door at ground level, and the metal-railed staircase on the left.
description_view_1: Displays the broad brick wall with two arched windows, signage, and utility fixtures; the staircase and side extension are partially visible.
description_view_2: Highlights the rear brick wall with a single arched window, a small vent, and the edge of the wooden extension.
description_view_3: Focuses on the wooden side extension with a sloped roof, a window, and the staircase leading to a secondary entrance.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical placement with arched and rectangular windows
attribute_entryway_design: Main entryway features a plain door at ground level accessed by a concrete staircase with a metal railing; no porch or decorative elements.
